title: About Me
text:
Hi, I’m Manasvi 👋
I’m a Master’s student in Business Analytics at UC Irvine with a background in Computer Science and Economics. I’m passionate about using data, machine learning, and AI to solve real-world business problems — especially in healthcare, pharmaceuticals, and emerging technologies.
I’ve worked on projects ranging from financial sentiment analysis and stock prediction models to predictive analytics and dashboard development. My experience includes Python, SQL, machine learning, and data visualization tools like Power BI and Tableau.
Beyond academics, I’m competitive by nature — I was captain of my college tennis team — which has shaped my leadership style, resilience, and ability to perform under pressure.
I’m currently seeking opportunities in data analytics and AI-driven strategy roles where I can combine technical depth with business impact.

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
This project explores how financial sentiment impacts stock behavior by comparing two popular NLP approaches:

FinBERT (domain-specific transformer model)
VADER (rule-based sentiment model)

The goal was to evaluate model robustness and predictive signal strength when applied to financial text datasets.

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
This project explores how financial sentiment impacts stock behavior by comparing two popular NLP approaches:

FinBERT (domain-specific transformer model)
VADER (rule-based sentiment model)

The goal was to evaluate model robustness and predictive signal strength when applied to financial text datasets.

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
🔍 Methodology

Cleaned and preprocessed financial text data
Generated sentiment scores using:

FinBERT
VADER

Compared predicted sentiment vs. actual market movement
Evaluated signal strength using correlation and regression analysis

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
📊 Key Findings

FinBERT showed stronger contextual understanding.
VADER performed well on clearly polarized statements but struggled with financial nuance.
Sentiment signal had moderate predictive power depending on volatility regime.

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
🛠 Tools Used

Python
HuggingFace Transformers
Pandas
Scikit-learn
Matplotlib

title: Financial Sentiment Analysis: Predicting Market Reactions
text:
📈 Next Steps

Test multi-day lag effects
Expand dataset to additional tickers
Explore ensemble sentiment modeling
